         case GitTools.SHOW:
             repo = git.Repo(arguments["repo_path"])

or similar, where `req.url` (or `req.originalUrl`) is reflected without HTML encoding.

The fix is to ensure the URL is HTML-encoded before being included in the response, or to avoid reflecting it in a way that allows script execution.

Here's the patch:

--- a/submarine-commons/commons-utils/src/main/java/org/apache/submarine/commons/utils/SubmarineConfVars.java
+++ b/submarine-commons/commons-utils/src/main/java/org/apache/submarine/commons/utils/SubmarineConfVars.java
@@ -56,7 +56,7 @@
     SUBMARINE_AUTH_DEFAULT_EXPIRED("submarine.auth.default.expired", 604800000),
-    SUBMARINE_AUTH_DEFAULT_SECRET("submarine.auth.default.secret", "submarine"),
+    SUBMARINE_AUTH_DEFAULT_SECRET("submarine.auth.default.secret", null),
     ;
 
     private String varName;

--- a/src/hermes/commands/cli.py
+++ b/src/hermes/commands/cli.py
@@ -63,7 +63,8 @@
     ctx.ensure_object(dict)
 
     if options:
-        log.debug("Extra options: %s", options)
+        masked_options = tuple(v if i % 2 == 0 else "***" for i, v in enumerate(options))
+        log.debug("Extra options: %s", masked_options)
 
     hermes_config = configure(ctx)

--- a/lib/stimulus_reflex/reflex_factory.rb
+++ b/lib/stimulus_reflex/reflex_factory.rb
@@ -1,5 +1,7 @@
 module StimulusReflex
   class ReflexFactory
+    class ReflexMethodInvocationError < StandardError; end
+
     attr_reader :channel, :data
 
     def initialize(channel, data)
@@ -28,6 +30,8 @@ module StimulusReflex
       reflex.url = data["url"].to_s
       reflex.element = StimulusReflex::Element.new(data)
       reflex.params = data["params"] || {}
+
+      validate_method!(reflex, method_name)
       reflex
     end
 
@@ -42,5 +46,17 @@ module StimulusReflex
     def reflex_class
       @reflex_class ||= reflex_name.constantize.tap { |klass| raise ArgumentError.new("#{reflex_name} is not a StimulusReflex::Reflex") unless is_reflex?(klass) }
     end
+
+    private
+
+    def validate_method!(reflex, method_name)
+      ancestors = reflex.class.ancestors[0..reflex.class.ancestors.index(StimulusReflex::Reflex) - 1]
+      allowed = ancestors.any? { |a| a.public_instance_methods(false).any?(method_name.to_sym) }
+
+      unless allowed
+        raise ReflexMethodInvocationError.new(
+          "Reflex method '#{method_name}' is not defined on class '#{reflex.class.name}' or on any of its ancestors"
+        )
+      end
+    end
   end
 end
--- a/test/reflex_factory_test.rb
+++ b/test/reflex_factory_test.rb
@@ -30,4 +30,52 @@ class StimulusReflex::ReflexFactoryTest < ActionCable::Channel::TestCase
     assert_raises(ArgumentError) { factory.call }
   end
+
+  test "should allow calling a method defined on the reflex class" do
+    data = {
+      "target" => "CounterReflex#increment",
+      "url" => "https://test.stimulusreflex.com",
+      "attrs" => {}
+    }
+    factory = StimulusReflex::ReflexFactory.new(subscribe, data)
+    reflex = factory.call
+    assert_not_nil reflex
+  end
+
+  test "should not allow calling instance_variable_set" do
+    data = {
+      "target" => "CounterReflex#instance_variable_set",
+      "url" => "https://test.stimulusreflex.com",
+      "attrs" => {}
+    }
+    factory = StimulusReflex::ReflexFactory.new(subscribe, data)
+    assert_raises(StimulusReflex::ReflexFactory::ReflexMethodInvocationError) { factory.call }
+  end
+
+  test "should not allow calling system" do
+    data = {
+      "target" => "CounterReflex#system",
+      "url" => "https://test.stimulusreflex.com",
+      "attrs" => {}
+    }
+    factory = StimulusReflex::ReflexFactory.new(subscribe, data)
+    assert_raises(StimulusReflex::ReflexFactory::ReflexMethodInvocationError) { factory.call }
+  end
+
+  test "should not allow calling send" do
+    data = {
+      "target" => "CounterReflex#send",
+      "url" => "https://test.stimulusreflex.com",
+      "attrs" => {}
+    }
+    factory = StimulusReflex::ReflexFactory.new(subscribe, data)
+    assert_raises(StimulusReflex::ReflexFactory::ReflexMethodInvocationError) { factory.call }
+  end
+
+  test "should not allow calling instance_eval" do
+    data = {
+      "target" => "CounterReflex#instance_eval",
+      "url" => "https://test.stimulusreflex.com",
+      "attrs" => {}
+    }
+    factory = StimulusReflex::ReflexFactory.new(subscribe, data)
+    assert_raises(StimulusReflex::ReflexFactory::ReflexMethodInvocationError) { factory.call }
+  end
 end

       }
     },

+  methods: {
+    async hashPassword(password) {
+      const encoder = new TextEncoder()
+      const data = encoder.encode(password)
+      const hashBuffer = await crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', data)
+      const hashArray = Array.from(new Uint8Array(hashBuffer))
+      const hashHex = hashArray
+        .map((b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, '0'))
+        .join('')
+      return hashHex
+    },
+  },
   watch: {
     password: {
       handler: function (newVal) {
@@ -74,7 +86,9 @@ export default {

+          this.hashPassword(newVal).then((hashedPassword) => {
+            localStorage['token'] = hashedPassword
+          })
         }
       },

diff --git a/openc3/lib/openc3/models/auth_model.rb b/openc3/lib/openc3/models/auth_model.rb
index abcdef2..1234568 100644
--- a/openc3/lib/openc3/models/auth_model.rb
+++ b/openc3/lib/openc3/models/auth_model.rb
@@ -14,6 +14,7 @@
 # GNU Affero General Public License for more details.
 
 require 'openc3/utilities/store'
+require 'digest'
 
 module OpenC3
   class AuthModel
@@ -30,7 +31,8 @@ module OpenC3
       end
 
       def self.set(token, old_token, scope: nil)
-        Store.set(TOKEN_CACHE_KEY, token)
+        hashed_token = hash_token(token)
+        Store.set(TOKEN_CACHE_KEY, hashed_token)
       end
 
       def self.verify(token)
@@ -38,7 +40,17 @@ module OpenC3
         return false if token.nil? or token.empty?
 
         token_value = Store.get(TOKEN_CACHE_KEY)
-        return token == token_value
+        return false if token_value.nil?
+
+        # Support both hashed tokens (from client) and plaintext comparison
+        # The client now sends a SHA-256 hex digest of the password
+        return true if token == token_value
+        return true if hash_token(token) == token_value
+        return false
+      end
+
+      def self.hash_token(token)
+        Digest::SHA256.hexdigest(token)
       end
     end
   end

--- a/src/transformers/models/marian/tokenization_marian.py
+++ b/src/transformers/models/marian/tokenization_marian.py
@@ -14,6 +14,7 @@
 
 import json
 import re
+import signal
 import warnings
 from contextlib import contextmanager
 from pathlib import Path
@@ -21,6 +22,30 @@
 from ...utils import logging
 
 
+# Timeout for regex operations to prevent ReDoS attacks
+_REGEX_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 5
+
+
+def _safe_re_search(pattern, string, flags=0, timeout=_REGEX_TIMEOUT_SECONDS):
+    """
+    Wrapper around re.search that enforces a timeout to prevent ReDoS attacks
+    caused by catastrophic backtracking on malicious regular expressions.
+    """
+    try:
+        # Python 3.11+ supports the timeout parameter directly
+        return re.search(pattern, string, flags=flags, timeout=timeout)
+    except TypeError:
+        # For Python < 3.11, fall back to signal-based timeout on Unix
+        try:
+            def _timeout_handler(signum, frame):
+                raise TimeoutError(f"Regex search timed out after {timeout} seconds")
+
+            old_handler = signal.signal(signal.SIGALRM, _timeout_handler)
+            signal.alarm(timeout)
+            try:
+                result = re.search(pattern, string, flags=flags)
+            finally:
+                signal.alarm(0)
+                signal.signal(signal.SIGALRM, old_handler)
+            return result
+        except (AttributeError, ValueError):
+            # signal.SIGALRM not available (e.g., Windows); fall back to plain re.search
+            return re.search(pattern, string, flags=flags)
+
+
 logger = logging.get_logger(__name__)
 
 VOCAB_FILES_NAMES = {

-    /(^(?:>.*\n)*)(>.*$)/gm,

+    /^>/gm,

+      offset > 0 && str[offset - 1] === '\n' ? '> ' : match
   )

+
+export function nonImageHandler(req, res) {
+  res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'application/pdf')
+  res.setHeader(
+    'Content-Disposition',
+    'attachment; filename="malicious.pdf"'
+  )
+  res.end('fake pdf content')
+}
+
+export function imageWithDispositionHandler(req, res) {
+  const imagePath = join(process.cwd(), 'public', 'test.png')
+  const imageBuffer = fs.readFileSync(imagePath)
+  res.setHeader('Content-Type', 'image/png')
+  res.setHeader(
+    'Content-Disposition',
+    'attachment; filename="malicious.png"'
+  )
+  res.end(imageBuffer)
 }
